title: Goodbyes — seven-name pre-Austin closeout list (Cameron Canfield → Liam Smyth)
html:
Goodbyes — seven-name pre-Austin closeout list

Source: 2025-06-09 Apple Note. Seven named goodbyes in a flat list, all distinct from the Comrades for Legacy cohort. Three months before the Austin move executes.

TL;DR

  • The seven goodbyes: Cameron Canfield, Raahi, Yoonie, Steven Sarber, Memphis 7/11, Arman Cuba Salsa Dancing, Yussef IHOUSE Kuwait, Liam Smyth.
  • The two compound entries are place-coded memories — "Memphis 7/11" and "Arman Cuba Salsa Dancing" and "Yussef IHOUSE Kuwait" — Yahir tagging the people by where the moment happened, CIEE / YES program travel-friend pattern.
  • Distinct from the Comrades: zero overlap. Comrades = keep forever; Goodbyes = honor the chapter and let close.
  • The pre-Austin closeout cohort — paired with the Aug 21 family side goodbye list (Tío Edgar, Grandpa Eddie, Kai). Two waves: friends in June, family in August.
  • Specimen of the end of chapter reflection genre in its most-utilitarian form — no narration, just names and place-tags.

Key quote

"Arman Cuba Salsa Dancing" — the entry that most clearly preserves a specific memory inside the name.

Pattern observed

The June friends-goodbye / August family-goodbye split mirrors the same two-wave structure Yahir used at the end of the China chapter — closeout the long-distance / cohort relationships first, then close out the immediate-orbit relationships right before departure. Two months between waves is the same cadence (Dec 2024 departing China saw a similar pattern).

The presence of CIEE / YES KL friends (Yussef, Arman, Memphis) suggests this list reaches further back than just college — it's clearing residue from the high-school international chapter too. Austin is being treated as a hard reset, not just a move.

Open questions

  • Did the actual goodbyes happen, or did some get skipped?
  • Are any of these names already-existing entities? (Owen-style fame check needed for Cameron Canfield, Liam Smyth.)
  • Memphis 7/11 — single visit, or a recurring location?
